tips_en: Start with the Program-as-Weights paper — its core result is striking (a 0.6B model outperforming a 32B one at 50× less memory) and the conceptual framing of fuzzy functions as compilable neural programs is both novel and practically useful for anyone deploying LLM-based code. The two agent papers (autonomous policy evolution and Slay the Spire 2 memory) pair well as a second read: together they offer complementary perspectives on agent design—one isolating feedback-driven self-improvement, the other dissecting long-horizon memory into typed, individually ablatable layers. As a nice cross-cutting link, the small efficient interpreter from Paper 1 could plausibly serve as a lightweight policy network inside the agent frameworks benchmarked in Papers 2 and 3.
tips_zh: 建议先读 Program-as-Weights 那篇——它的核心结果非常亮眼（0.6B 模型以 50 倍更少的显存击败 32B 模型），把"模糊函数"建模为可编译神经程序这一概念既新颖又对实际部署 LLM 编程极具价值。另外两篇智能体论文（自主策略演化与 Slay the Spire 2 记忆架构）适合放在一起读：前者聚焦反馈驱动的自我改进，后者将长程记忆拆解为可逐层消融的类型化组件，二者形成互补视角。额外值得一提的是，Paper 1 编译出的高效小模型理论上也可作为 Papers 2 和 3 中智能体框架的轻量级策略/解释器，形成跨篇的连接。
